data.title: Leh Nubra Pangong (Ex Leh) 5 Nights - 6 Days (Copy) Test
data.greeting_message:
Hello Manvinder

Welcome to the start of an unforgettable journey!

We are truly delighted that you have chosen to travel with us. This itinerary has been thoughtfully designed to ensure you experience the very best of your destination — from remarkable sights and cultural highlights to moments of relaxation and discovery.

info_sections.title: Inclusions
info_sections.content:
• Accommodation in specified hotels.
  • Daily breakfast at the hotel.
  • All transfers and sightseeing by private AC vehicle.
  • Driver allowance, tolls, and parking fees.
  • All applicable taxes (GST).
  • Airport/railway station pick-up and drop-off.
  • Entrance fees to specified monuments.
  • Services of a professional tour guide on specified days.
  • Welcome drink on arrival at hotels.
  • One complimentary mineral water bottle per person per day.

info_sections.title: Exclusions
info_sections.content:
• Airfare or train fare to/from the starting point.
  • Any meals not mentioned in the itinerary.
  • Expenses of a personal nature like tips, laundry, telephone calls, etc.
  • Any adventure activity costs, camera fees, etc.
  • Medical and travel insurance.
  • Any cost arising due to natural calamities like landslides, road blocks, etc.
  • Any increase in taxes or fuel price, leading to increase in cost.
  • Early check-in or late check-out charges at hotels.
  • Anything not specifically mentioned in the "Inclusions" column.
  • Visa charges, if applicable.

info_sections.title: Terms & Conditions
info_sections.content:
1. Booking & Deposits: A contract is formed upon written confirmation and payment of a required deposit to secure the reservation.
  2. Final Payment: The remaining balance must be paid in full by a specified deadline (e.g., 60 days before travel) or the booking may be cancelled.
  3. Cancellations: Fees are applied on a sliding scale; the closer you cancel to the departure date, the higher the penalty.
  4. Third-Party Liability: The agency acts as an intermediary and is not responsible for the errors, delays, or defaults of airlines, hotels, or transport providers.
  5. Travel Insurance: Clients are strongly advised to purchase comprehensive insurance to cover medical emergencies, trip cancellations, and lost luggage.
  6. Documentation: Travelers are solely responsible for ensuring their passports, visas, and health certificates are valid and meet entry requirements.
  7. Itinerary Changes: The agency reserves the right to modify routes or substitute hotels of similar quality due to safety, weather, or operational needs.
  8. Force Majeure: No liability is accepted for disruptions caused by "Acts of God," war, strikes, pandemics, or government-imposed restrictions.
  9. Price Adjustments: Quotes are subject to change before final payment due to currency fluctuations, tax increases, or fuel surcharges.
  10. Dispute Resolution: Any legal claims will be governed by the laws of the agency’s registered jurisdiction and settled through local courts or arbitration.

info_sections.title: Refund & Cancellation Policy
info_sections.content:
1. Written Notification Required: All cancellation requests must be submitted in writing via email to the agency. Verbal or WhatsApp messages are generally not considered valid proof of cancellation.
  2. Staggered Cancellation Fees: Charges are usually based on how far in advance you cancel. For example, cancelling 30+ days prior might incur a 25% fee, while 15–29 days could be 50%, and less than 15 days often results in no refund.
  3. Non-Refundable Deposits: Initial booking deposits (often 25–40% of the total cost) are typically non-refundable once the booking is confirmed with vendors.
  4. Airlines and Third-Party Policies: Refunds for air tickets or specific hotel brands are governed by their respective terms. The agency may also charge a separate service fee for processing these third-party cancellations.
  5. No-Show Policy: If a traveler fails to show up for a flight, tour, or hotel check-in without prior notice, they are typically charged 100% of the booking cost with no eligibility for a refund.
  6. Peak Season Exceptions: Different, often stricter, rules apply during peak travel periods (e.g., Christmas, New Year, or local festivals), where bookings may become entirely non-refundable regardless of the notice period.
  7. Force Majeure Protection: The agency is generally not liable for refunds due to "Acts of God," such as natural disasters, pandemics, or political unrest, though they may offer credit vouchers for future use.
  8. Refund Processing Time: Once a refund is approved, it typically takes 7–14 business days to process, though banking procedures for international transfers can sometimes take 2–7 weeks.
  9. Original Payment Method: Refunds are almost always credited back to the original mode of payment (e.g., the specific credit card used) and cannot be paid out in cash to a different person.
  10. Unused Services: No partial refunds are provided for services that are part of a package but not used by the traveler, such as missed meals, early hotel check-outs, or skipped sightseeing tours.

info_sections.title: Payment policy
info_sections.content:
1. Initial Deposit: A non-refundable deposit (typically 25–50% of the total tour cost) is required at the time of booking to secure reservations.
  2. Balance Due Date: The remaining balance must be settled at least 30 days (for international) or 15 days (for domestic) prior to the departure date.
  3. Last-Minute Bookings: For any reservations made within 15–30 days of the travel date, full payment (100%) is required immediately upon confirmation.
  4. Peak Season Requirements: Bookings during "High Peak" periods (e.g., Dec 20 – Jan 5, major festivals) often require 100% advance payment at the time of booking.
  5. Accepted Payment Methods: Payments are generally accepted via Credit/Debit cards, Bank Transfers (NEFT/IMPS/Wire), Digital Wallets (UPI/Apple Pay/Google Pay), and sometimes Cheques.
  6. Surcharges & Fees: Payments made via credit or debit cards may incur additional processing fees (typically 2–5%) levied by the bank or payment gateway.
  7. Currency & Exchange Rates: All quotes are typically in a base currency (e.g., USD or INR). Payments made in other currencies will be calculated based on the prevailing exchange rate on the day of the transaction.
  8. Late Payment Penalty: Failure to pay the balance by the due date may result in a late fee (e.g., 2% monthly) or the automatic cancellation of the booking with forfeiture of the deposit.
  9. Price Volatility: Prices are only guaranteed once the full payment is received. Agencies reserve the right to adjust quotes for any government tax hikes or fuel surcharges before final payment.
  10. Installment Plans: Some agencies offer "Buy Now, Pay Later" (BNPL) or structured installment plans for high-value packages, subject to a formal credit agreement.

info_sections.title: Amendment Policy
info_sections.content:
1. Written Requests: All change requests (dates, hotels, or names) must be submitted in writing via email to be officially processed.
  2. Amendment Fees: The agency usually charges a standard administrative fee (e.g., ₹1,000 - ₹5,000 or $50 - $100) per change, in addition to any vendor costs.
  3. Availability Dependent: All requested changes are subject to availability at the time of the request and cannot be guaranteed.
  4. Fare Differences: If an amendment results in a higher price (e.g., moving to a more expensive hotel or peak date), the traveler must pay the difference in cost.
  5. Non-Transferable Bookings: Many bookings (especially flight tickets) are non-transferable to another person due to airline security and name-change restrictions.
  6. Timeline Restrictions: Amendments made very close to the departure date (e.g., within 7–14 days) may be treated as a cancellation and re-booking, incurring higher fees.
  7. Third-Party Costs: Any fees charged by airlines, hotels, or transport providers for changes will be passed directly to the traveler.
  8. Reduction in Group Size: If an amendment reduces the number of travelers, the remaining passengers may face a price increase to cover shared costs (like private vehicles).
  9. Downgrade Policy: If a change results in a lower-priced package, the agency may not refund the difference if the original services were already locked in and paid for.
  10. Limit on Changes: Agencies often limit the number of free or low-cost amendments; beyond a certain point (e.g., 2 changes), they may charge additional service fees.

info_sections.title: Why Choose Us
info_sections.content:
• Expertly Crafted Itineraries: Designed by local experts who know the hidden gems, not just the tourist traps.
  • Tailored to You: Completely customizable, whether you want a packed adventure or a relaxing getaway.
  • 24/7 Support: We are with you every step of the way, offering peace of mind from departure to return.
  • Curated Experiences: Access to unique, off-the-beaten-path activities you won't find in a guidebook.

components.name: Note
components.description: Have an early breakfast today morning and leave for the Shyam Valley Excursion. First place visit Hall of Fame. Then you will be be visiting the renowned Gurudwara Pathar Sahib. The biggest attraction of the day, is the visit to the Magnetic Hill, where you will see your car move uphill even when in neutral gear. Later visit the confluence or Sangam Point of Indus and Zanskar (Sangam), a colorful experience. Evening visit to Shanti Stupa built in 1991 by Japanese Buddhist Bhikshu Gyomyo Nakamura. Shanti stupa provides beautiful and panoramic view of the surrounding landscape. Shanti Stupa in Ladakh was built to promote world peace and a symbol of the ties between Japan and Ladakh.Overnight in leh.

components.name: Hall of Fame
components.description: The Hall of Fame Leh is a museum that is constructed and maintained by the Indian army. Hall of fame is located near the Leh Airfield. This museum is built in memory of the soldiers who had lost their lives through the Indo-Pak wars.

details.activityDescription: The Hall of Fame Leh is a museum that is constructed and maintained by the Indian army. Hall of fame is located near the Leh Airfield. This museum is built in memory of the soldiers who had lost their lives through the Indo-Pak wars.

components.name: Gurudwara Shri Pathar Sahib
components.description: Gurdwara Shri Pathar Sahib is situated on the Srinagar-Leh Road, 25kms before the Leh. It is very beautiful and it was created in memory of the founder of Sikh religion and the first guru , Guru Nanak Dev ji.

components.name: Note
components.description: Enjoy a beautiful morning in the camps, charge yourself with morning breakfast and create lifetime memories. Today, drive back to Leh driving on the same route crossing the Changla La (pass). Arrive at Leh around afternoon and check in at hotel. You can go for some shopping in nearby local markets or just relax. Have dinner and go for a relaxed sleep to recharge yourself for the upcoming day.

components.name: Welcome to nubara vally
components.description: On your arrival at Leh Airport, meet the local tour representative at specified area who will transfer you to the hotel. Take rest as youll need time to acclimatise in this climate. From low elevation at north or south reign of India, youve reached over 11000 feet and thus the weather is very different here. During afternoon, you can take a leisurely walk through the old town for a self-orientation with the lovely city.. Enjoy a comfortable overnight stay at the hotel in Leh

components.daywise_title: Day 1 Arrive at Leh

components.name: Some additional Note
components.description: Today after breakfast visit Hall of Fame, Gurudwara Pathar Sahib, Magnetic Hill & Sangam View where Indus & Zanskar river meet (confluence). Later we shall move back to Leh. Later visit Leh Palace and enjoy a sunset at Shanti Stupa. Night stay at hotel in Leh.
components.daywise_title: Day 1 Arrive at Leh

components.name: Note
components.description: After morning breakfast, get ready for a thrill filled day. Check out from the hotel and drive to Nubra Valley via Worlds Highest Motorable Pass. Drive through steep serpentine roads and reach Khardung La at a height of 5550 metres. Take a stop at the pass and enjoy birds eye view of Leh city. Capture the fantastic views in your camera before driving down to Nubra Valley. It is an enticing old caravan route at the confluence of Shyok and Nubra rivers. Arrive at the camp resort, freshen up and proceed to visit the charming valley of Diskit, the tiny capital of Nubra. Visit the famous Diskit Gompa, the oldest and largest Buddhist monastery (gompa) in the Nubra Valley providing a breath taking view of the valley. After the visit, further drive to Hunder to see the cold desert and the world famous two humped camels. Enjoy a unique camel ride (on your own cost). After a super exciting time, it is time for some rest. We get back to the camps in late afternoon and relax. Take leisurely strolls in the evening, have dinner and retire for a relaxed sleep
components.daywise_title: Day 03 LEH KHARDUNGLA PASS NUBRA VALLEY (125 KM / 5-6 Hrs)

components.name: Nubra Valley
components.description: Nubra Valley, known as the "Valley of Flowers" (Ldumra) or "Valley of Orchard," is a high-altitude cold desert in northern Ladakh, nestled between the Karakoram and Ladakh ranges at ~3,100m. Accessible via the world-renowned Khardung La pass (~18,380 ft), it is famous for its surreal sand dunes, double-humped Bactrian camels, Diskit monastery’s 108-ft Maitreya Buddha, and the Siachen Glacier.

details.activityDescription: Nubra Valley, known as the "Valley of Flowers" (Ldumra) or "Valley of Orchard," is a high-altitude cold desert in northern Ladakh, nestled between the Karakoram and Ladakh ranges at ~3,100m. Accessible via the world-renowned Khardung La pass (~18,380 ft), it is famous for its surreal sand dunes, double-humped Bactrian camels, Diskit monastery’s 108-ft Maitreya Buddha, and the Siachen Glacier.

components.daywise_title: Day 03 LEH KHARDUNGLA PASS NUBRA VALLEY (125 KM / 5-6 Hrs)

components.name: Note
components.description: Have an early breakfast today morning and set out for a visit to the famous Pangong Lake via Shyok. Enjoy the spectacular journey with amazing sceneries of snow clad Himalayas. Situated along India/China border, Pangong Lake is an utter delight to eyes with its various water colours seen at a stretch. It is the highest salt-water lake in the world with a height of 4267 metres, 130 kms long and about 6 kms wide. The perfectly clear environment with beautiful sky and surrounding hills add to the magnificence. Have your dinner, spend some more time with nature and enjoy the crystal clear sky views with glittering stars at night. Overnight in Pangong.
components.daywise_title: Day 04 NUBRA PANGONG LAKE VIA SHYOK (275 KM / 6 7 HRS)

components.name: Pangong Lake
components.description: Pangong Tso or Pangong Lake is an endorheic lake spanning eastern Ladakh and West Tibet situated at an elevation of 4,225 m. It is 134 km long.

components.daywise_title: Day 04 NUBRA PANGONG LAKE VIA SHYOK (275 KM / 6 7 HRS)

components.name: Note
components.description: Enjoy a beautiful morning in the camps, charge yourself with morning breakfast and create lifetime memories. Today, drive back to Leh driving on the same route crossing the Changla La (pass). Arrive at Leh around afternoon and check in at hotel. You can go for some shopping in nearby local markets or just relax. Have dinner and go for a relaxed sleep to recharge yourself for the upcoming day.
components.daywise_title: Day 05: PANGONG LEH VIA CHANGLA PASS (230 KM / 5 -6 HRS)

components.name: Chang La Pass
components.description: Chang La Pass is a high-altitude mountain pass in Ladakh, India, located at 5,360 meters (17,585-17,688 feet), acting as the gateway to the Changthang plateau and Pangong Lake. Known as one of the world's highest motorable roads, it is famous for its steep, rugged terrain, panoramic snow-capped views, and the nearby Changla Baba temple.
